commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers include contributor-owned live Cursor proof and maintainer acceptance of a new cookie-authenticated dashboard API; no repair marker should run before proof is...; Needs attention: The diff has no new dependency or workflow supply-chain issue, but the new CLI Cursor cost path can bypass the user's credential-source setting.; Review finding: [P1] Honor Curso... (sha=baacfb506606a42e4afdfa43e50ee0d07e39cd85)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Cursor dashboard-backed token-cost fetching, Cursor-metered totals in CLI/menu output, provider capability wiring, and focused Cursor cost tests across core, CLI, and UI surfaces.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection of PR head shows the Cursor CLI cost path lacks a settings/off gate, Cursor snapshots use the latest entry while UI/CLI labels say Today, and the inline dashboard can fall back to local-log copy. No live Cursor account probe was run because repository policy warns against credential-backed browser-cookie validation without explicit request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers include contributor-owned live Cursor proof and maintainer acceptance of a new cookie-authenticated dashboard API; no repair marker should run before proof is supplied.

target.title: feat(cursor): add token cost report with Cursor-metered total
target.body: ## Summary Adds a Cursor token-cost report to CodexBar, sourced from Cursor's cookie-authenticated dashboard API and reusing the existing Cursor session resolution (the same auth path as the status probe). Each usage event carries both a vendor list-price token cost (`tokenUsage.totalCents`) and the amount Cursor's plan actually deducts (`chargedCents`), so a single fetch yields both an API-rate per-day/per-model breakdown and a "Cursor-metered" window total covering the exact same window. ### Core - `CursorUsageEventsFetcher`: pages `POST /api/dashboard/get-filtered-usage-events`, dedupes events on their natural key, and shapes them into a `CostUsageDailyReport` (API-rate) plus a metered window total (sum of `chargedCents`). Lenient numeric decoding handles Cursor serializing some numbers as strings, and the CSRF-protected POST sends the required `Origin` header. - `CursorStatusProbe`: generalized `resolveSession` so status and cost share one session-resolution flow (manual cookie, cached cookie, browser cookies, stored session, Cursor.app fallback); added `fetchCostReport` on top of it. - `CostUsageFetcher`: macOS-only Cursor branch plus a `fetchAllHistory` option (all-time window and an "All time" label when set). - Enabled the Cursor `tokenCost` capability and added a Cursor-specific cost-estimate hint. ### CLI - `codexbar cost --provider cursor`, honoring `--days` (1…365) and a new `--all` flag for full history. - Text output adds a `Cursor-metered: $X (window)` line; JSON adds `meteredCostUSD`. The unsupported-provider error now lists supported providers dynamically. ### App (UI) - Menu cost card renders the `Cursor-metered` line alongside the API-rate estimate. - Preferences ▸ General gains a global "Fetch full Cursor cost history" toggle (`cursorFetchAllCostHistory`), wired through the settings store, observation, and the `UsageStore` refresh scope so toggling triggers a re-fetch. ## Tests - `CursorUsageEventsFetcherTests`: pagination/dedupe, metered-cents summing (including nil-vs-zero), and per-day/per-model report mapping using a stubbed transport. - `CursorStatusProbeTests`: cost-report path over the shared session resolution. ## Commands run - `swift build --product CodexBarCLI` — clean. - `swift build --product CodexBar` — clean (full app module compiles and links). - I could not run SwiftLint/SwiftFormat or the `Testing`-based suite locally: this machine has only CommandLineTools, which lacks the `Testing` framework and the SwiftUI macro plugins (`#Preview`, `@Entry`). Relying on CI for lint and the sharded macOS test suite. ## Screenshots ## Notes - Cost is fetched over the network from Cursor's dashboard API using existing cookie-based auth, consistent with other network-backed providers (e.g. Bedrock). No new dependencies.
target.author: eclinick

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
